_note: Prose-only skill (no verdict emoji) — the first of the judge-heavy tier (ADR-0022: these land second because the deterministic assert does little work). Rubric from sentinel/fixtures/coverage-review/expected-findings.md against refund.spec.js + refund.js: one green happy-path test with two loose assertions that leaves every guard, error path, and the full-refund boundary untested. The one deterministic token is the required mode line (SKILL step 3) — in this fixture there is no coverage output, so a faithful run states `static inference`. Real discrimination rests on must_surface (the specific gaps) / must_not (boundary), graded by the LLM judge; the hollow sample is the signature failure this skill exists to counter — a rubber-stamp `looks good` review that flags none of them. `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly.

must_surface.claim: Flags the loose assertions: `expect(result).toBeDefined()` asserts nothing about correctness, and `expect(result.ok).toBeTruthy()` never checks `status` or the computed `remaining`, so wrong arithmetic would still pass.

Flags the untested guard/error paths: no test rejects a non-positive amount (`amount <= 0` throw) or an over-refund (`amount > order.total` throw), and the already-`refunded` no-op branch is never exercised.
Flags the untested full-refund boundary: `remaining === 0` → `status: 'refunded'` is never hit (only the `'partial'` side is tested), and the `remaining` value is never asserted.

must_not.claim: Must NOT propose deleting or removing tests — coverage-review is the additive gap-finder; removing tests is `/prune-tests`.
Must NOT prove by mutation or run the suite — it reads coverage or infers statically; proving by mutation is `/audit-test`, and it never runs the suite to generate coverage (SKILL notes).
